methodology: Premium samples pulled from public quote pages on PolicyBazaar, JoinDitto, Beshak, and insurer rate cards (Tata AIG MediCare Premier official rate chart, Star Family Health Optima Annexure-2, Niva Bupa ReAssure 2.0 rate card) on 2026-05-13. Single quotes are illustrative starting premiums for healthy non-smoking lives in Delhi (metro / Zone 1 / Zone A); actual premium varies with underwriting, pin code, family composition, and add-ons. Where a specific (insurer, policy, age, SI) combination did not surface a public number, the closest comparable insurer band was used and the gap was filled with the scaling factors below (derived from the 2-3 anchor points that ARE real). Scaling factors are calibrated against the IRDAI bandings used by most Indian health insurers and are consistent with the visible deltas in the JoinDitto / Beshak premium charts. | PED-specialty plans (HDFC Energy): individual base anchor × ~1.35 PED load. Group/B2B plans (Aditya Birla Group Activ Health): individual base × ~0.60 group-rate factor.

notes.verified_anchor_points: 9 insurer/policy combinations have at least one premium value sourced from a real visible quote page (PolicyBazaar listing tile, JoinDitto chart, Tata AIG official rate chart, Star Health Annexure-2). Other points in each policy's samples[] array are interpolated from those anchors using the scaling_factors below; they are clearly marked source_url='derived_from_anchor' so the bot does not over-claim precision.
notes.currency: INR, annual premium, GST INCLUDED (18% as of 2026-05-13) unless explicitly tagged ex_tax in a sample

scaling_factors._baseline: All multipliers are relative to: age 30 / sum_insured Rs 5,00,000 / city_tier metro / non-smoker / family_size 1 (individual). The reference base premium across mainstream insurers at this profile is approximately Rs 5,000-6,500/yr in 2026 (e.g., HDFC Optima Secure Rs 4,800; Care Supreme Rs 4,800; Tata AIG MediCare Premier Rs 8,362).
scaling_factors._age_source: Derived from Tata AIG MediCare Premier official rate chart (19-35 / 5L = Rs 8,362; 36-45 / 5L = Rs 9,868 => 1.18x for that band, but the 46-55 band step is steep). Cross-checked against JoinDitto's HDFC Ergo Optima Secure and Niva Bupa ReAssure 2.0 published bands. Senior bands (56+) verified against Star Red Carpet and Bajaj Silver Health.
scaling_factors._si_source: Derived from Tata AIG MediCare Premier rate chart (5L = Rs 8,362; 10L = Rs 10,319 => 1.234x) and HDFC Optima Secure (5L Rs 4,800; 10L Rs 6,492 => 1.35x). 1.35x is the rounder average used here. Premium grows sub-linearly with SI because the catastrophic-tail probability is roughly log-linear in SI.
scaling_factors._city_source: Derived from Star Health zone-wise rate chart (Zone A vs B vs C) and Care/HDFC tier pricing differential mentioned across PolicyBazaar listings. Most insurers use 3 zones: Zone A (metros) ~ 1.00x, Zone B (tier-1) ~ 0.90-0.93x, Zone C (smaller cities) ~ 0.80-0.85x.
scaling_factors._smoker_source: Acko, PolicyBazaar smoker articles: typical loading 15-25% for declared regular smokers; some insurers go up to 30-40% for heavy smokers (>10 cigs/day). 1.25x chosen as mid-point for the bot's typical conservative quote.
scaling_factors._floater_source: Derived from comparison of individual vs 1A+1C, 2A+0C, 2A+1C, 2A+2C rates in Star Family Health Optima Annexure-2 and Niva Bupa Health Premia rate card. A family floater for 4 is ~2.4x an individual policy of the eldest member at the same SI, not 4x, because the SI is shared.
scaling_factors._combined_example: 30-year-old non-smoker in metro, 10L SI, family floater for 4: base ~Rs 5,500 (HDFC anchor) * age 1.0 * SI 1.35 * city 1.0 * smoker 1.0 * floater 2.4 = ~Rs 17,820/year. Cross-check: HDFC Optima Secure family floater 2A+2C 10L in Delhi typically quoted at Rs 16,000-20,000 on PolicyBazaar.
scaling_factors.rounding: All computed premiums should be rounded to nearest Rs 100 when surfaced to the user. Use these as a soft estimate; always nudge the user toward a formal quote on the insurer's portal.

ped_specialty_load._source: Acko + PolicyBazaar published comparisons of PED-specialty vs base health plans 2024; range 30-40%, median 35%

group_plan_discount._source: Cafemutual + IRDAI group health filings; group rates typically 50-70% of individual
